result: If I could change the world in one way, I would ensure every person has consistent access to high-quality education throughout their life. Education—attuned to cultural context, centered on equity, and continuously adaptable—could empower people to make informed decisions, innovate solutions, and participate fully in shaping society. With more people equipped to critically understand their world, the ripple effects might include healthier democracies, more resilient economies, scientific breakthroughs, and more compassionate communities.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
